exam.title: MSc Admission Test Preparation
instructions: Answer all fifty questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
There is no negative marking. The qualifying mark is 60 (40%).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A square matrix is invertible if and only if:

options.c: it has a row of zeros

questions.explanation: A square matrix is invertible exactly when its determinant is nonzero (equivalently, it has full rank).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What are the roots of the quadratic equation $x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0$?
questions.explanation: Factoring gives (x − 2)(x − 3) = 0, so the roots are 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is $\frac{d}{dx}(x^3)$?
questions.explanation: By the power rule, d/dx(xⁿ) = n·xⁿ⁻¹, so d/dx(x³) = 3x².

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the sum of the first n natural numbers, 1 + 2 + … + n?
questions.explanation: The arithmetic series 1 + 2 + … + n equals n(n+1)/2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If $\log_2 32 = x$, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: 32 = 2⁵, so log₂ 32 = 5.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: A fair six-sided die is rolled once. What is the probability of getting an even number?
questions.explanation: Even outcomes {2, 4, 6} number 3 out of 6, so the probability is 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: What is the mode of the data set 2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 3, 2?
questions.explanation: The value 3 appears three times, more than any other, so the mode is 3.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: The Pearson correlation coefficient always lies in which range?
questions.explanation: Pearson's r ranges from −1 (perfect negative) through 0 (none) to +1 (perfect positive correlation).

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: In the Chomsky hierarchy, which grammar type generates exactly the regular languages?
questions.explanation: Type 3 (regular) grammars generate the regular languages; Type 2 are context-free, Type 1 context-sensitive, Type 0 unrestricted.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: Over the alphabet {a, b}, the regular expression (a + b)* denotes:

options.b: all strings over {a, b}, including the empty string

questions.explanation: (a + b)* is the Kleene star of any single symbol, generating every string over {a, b}, the empty string included.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: A language is decidable (recursive) if:

options.a: some Turing machine accepts at least one of its strings
options.b: there is a Turing machine that halts on every input and decides membership
options.d: it is generated by a regular expression

questions.explanation: Decidability requires a total Turing machine that always halts and answers yes/no for membership.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: How many final (accepting) states can a DFA have?
questions.explanation: A DFA may have any number of accepting states, including none (which accepts the empty language).

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the output of the following C code?
questions.explanation: 4 is 100 in binary; ANDing with 1 (001) gives 0, so 4 is even.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What does this C code print?
questions.explanation: Printing a char with %d shows its ASCII code; 'A' is 65.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: Which statement about references in C++ is correct?

options.b: A reference must be initialized when it is declared
options.c: A reference can later be reseated to another object
options.d: A reference and a pointer are identical

questions.explanation: A C++ reference must be bound to an object at declaration and cannot be reseated or be null.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What does the following Java statement print?
questions.explanation: Evaluation is left to right: 10 + 20 = 30 (numeric), then 30 + "30" concatenates to "3030".

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the output of this C program?
questions.explanation: `a = 0` is an assignment whose value is 0 (false), so the else branch runs and prints "nonzero".

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Heap sort has a worst-case running time of:
questions.explanation: Heap sort builds a heap in O(n) and then does n extractions costing O(log n) each, giving O(n log n) overall.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which traversal of a binary search tree visits the keys in sorted ascending order?
questions.explanation: Inorder traversal (left, root, right) of a BST yields keys in increasing order.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: In a min-heap, the smallest element is always located at:
questions.explanation: The min-heap property guarantees the minimum is at the root.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: How many comparisons are needed, in the worst case, to find the maximum of n elements in an unsorted array?
questions.explanation: Scanning once and tracking the running maximum needs n − 1 comparisons.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: An adjacency-matrix representation of a graph with V vertices uses how much space?
questions.explanation: An adjacency matrix stores a V×V grid of entries, so it needs O(V²) space regardless of edge count.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which of the following problems is classically solved with dynamic programming?

options.a: Finding the minimum element of an array

questions.explanation: The 0/1 knapsack problem has overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure, solved efficiently with DP.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: Which CPU component performs arithmetic and logical operations?
questions.explanation: The ALU carries out arithmetic (add, subtract) and logic (AND, OR, NOT) operations.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: A key feature of the von Neumann architecture is that it stores:

options.b: instructions and data in the same memory

questions.explanation: In the von Neumann (stored-program) model, instructions and data share the same memory.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: What is the decimal value of the binary number 1010?

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: A processor with a 32-bit address bus can directly address at most:
questions.explanation: 2³² = 4,294,967,296 distinct byte addresses = 4 GB of addressable memory.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: The act of saving one process's CPU state and loading another's is called:
questions.explanation: A context switch saves the running process's registers/PC and restores those of the next scheduled process.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Round Robin CPU scheduling is based on:
questions.explanation: Round Robin gives each ready process a fixed time slice (quantum) in cyclic order.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Which memory-management scheme is prone to external fragmentation?
questions.explanation: Variable-size segments leave scattered free holes between allocations, causing external fragmentation; paging avoids it.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: A zombie process is one that:

options.b: has terminated but still has an entry in the process table awaiting its parent's wait()

questions.explanation: A zombie has finished execution but its PCB lingers until the parent reaps its exit status with wait().

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: In UNIX, what does the fork() system call return to the child process on success?
questions.explanation: fork() returns 0 in the child and the child's PID in the parent, letting each branch distinguish itself.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which protocol is used to transfer email from a client to a mail server (sending mail)?
questions.explanation: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) sends/relays outgoing email; IMAP and POP3 retrieve it.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: How many usable host addresses are available in a /24 (255.255.255.0) subnet?
questions.explanation: 2⁸ = 256 addresses minus the network and broadcast addresses leaves 254 usable hosts.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: The Domain Name System (DNS) primarily translates:
questions.explanation: DNS resolves human-readable domain names (e.g. example.com) into IP addresses.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: The TCP three-way handshake uses which sequence of control flags?
questions.explanation: The client sends SYN, the server replies SYN-ACK, and the client confirms with ACK to establish the connection.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: The 'Durability' property of a transaction guarantees that:

options.a: transactions are isolated from one another
options.b: once committed, changes survive subsequent system failures

questions.explanation: Durability means committed results are permanently recorded and persist even after a crash.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which SQL join returns only the rows that have matching values in both tables?
questions.explanation: An inner join returns only rows where the join condition matches in both tables.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Creating an index on a database column primarily improves:
questions.explanation: An index speeds up lookups and range scans, though it adds overhead to inserts/updates.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: In an Entity-Relationship (ER) diagram, an entity is represented by a:
questions.explanation: Rectangles denote entities; ellipses denote attributes and diamonds denote relationships.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: Replicating data across multiple nodes mainly improves:
questions.explanation: Replication lets the system serve requests and survive failures even if some replicas are down, improving availability.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: A stateless server is one that:

options.b: retains no per-client session state between requests

questions.explanation: A stateless server treats each request independently, holding no client context between requests, which eases scaling.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: Eventual consistency means that:

options.a: all replicas are always identical at every instant
options.b: if no new updates are made, all replicas eventually converge to the same value
options.d: every read returns the latest write immediately

questions.explanation: Under eventual consistency, replicas may diverge briefly but converge once updates stop propagating.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: A single-layer perceptron can correctly classify only problems that are:
questions.explanation: A perceptron learns a linear decision boundary, so it handles only linearly separable data (XOR is not).

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Gradient descent is an optimization method used to:
questions.explanation: Gradient descent iteratively updates parameters in the direction that reduces the loss function.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: The k-Nearest Neighbors algorithm classifies a new point based on:

options.a: the global centroid of all data
options.b: the majority class among its k closest training points

questions.explanation: k-NN assigns the class that is most common among the k nearest labelled neighbours by distance.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: In reinforcement learning, an agent learns primarily from:

options.a: a fixed set of labelled examples
options.b: rewards and penalties received by interacting with an environment

questions.explanation: A reinforcement-learning agent improves its policy using reward signals from its actions in the environment.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: Find the odd one out: Dog, Cat, Lion, Rose.
questions.explanation: Dog, cat and lion are animals; rose is a flower, so it is the odd one out.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: If each letter is coded by its position in the alphabet (A=1, B=2, …), then 'DOG' is coded as:
questions.explanation: D is the 4th letter, O the 15th, and G the 7th, giving 4-15-7.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: What is the angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at exactly 3:00?
questions.explanation: At 3:00 the minute hand is at 12 and the hour hand at 3, three hour-marks apart × 30° = 90°.
